title: Power BI Fundamentals
description: Step into the world of Business Intelligence. Learn how to connect to data sources, transform data using Power Query, build data models, and create interactive visualizations. This course is the perfect starting point for aspiring data analysts.

seo_metadata.title: Power BI Fundamentals Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Learn Microsoft Power BI from scratch. Master data connectivity, Power Query, DAX basics, and interactive reporting.

learning_outcomes: Connect Power BI Desktop to various data sources (Excel, CSV, Web)
Clean and transform data using the Power Query Editor
Create introductory DAX calculations (Calculated Columns and Measures)
Design and publish interactive reports and dashboards

modules.title: Data Connectivity and Power Query
modules.description: How to get your data into Power BI and clean it.

lessons.title: Introduction to Power BI Desktop
lessons.content:
The Power BI Ecosystem

Power BI consists of Power BI Desktop (for authoring), Power BI Service (for sharing), and Power BI Mobile. We will focus on Desktop.

lessons.title: Connecting to Data
lessons.content:
Getting Data

You can connect to hundreds of data sources. We will start by connecting to a standard Excel workbook and a CSV file using the 'Get Data' button.

lessons.title: Power Query Editor
lessons.content:
Cleaning Data

Data is rarely perfect. Use Power Query to remove blanks, change data types, split columns, and merge queries before loading the data into your model.

modules.title: Data Modeling and DAX Basics
modules.description: Structuring your data and creating calculations.

lessons.title: Building a Data Model
lessons.content:
Relationships

A data model connects multiple tables using relationships (1-to-Many). This allows you to slice sales data by dates, products, or regions without merging everything into one giant table.

lessons.title: Introduction to DAX
lessons.content:
Data Analysis Expressions

DAX is the formula language of Power BI. It is similar to Excel formulas but works on entire columns or tables instead of individual cells.

lessons.title: Calculated Columns vs. Measures
lessons.content:
Understanding the Difference

A Calculated Column evaluates row by row. A Measure evaluates based on the current filter context (e.g., total sales for a specific year). Measures are preferred for aggregations.

lessons.title: Creating Visuals
lessons.content:
Charts and Graphs

Drag and drop fields onto the canvas to create Bar Charts, Line Charts, and Matrices. Customize colors, labels, and titles in the Format pane.

lessons.title: Interactive Filtering
lessons.content:
Cross-Filtering

By default, selecting a bar in one chart will filter or highlight the data in all other charts on the page. Learn how to edit these interactions.

lessons.title: Publishing to Power BI Service
lessons.content:
Sharing Your Work

Once your report is complete, publish it to the cloud-based Power BI Service so your team can view and interact with the dashboard via a web browser.

mcqs.question: Which tool in Power BI is primarily used for cleaning and transforming data?
Which component of Power BI is the primary authoring tool for creating reports?
What is the primary difference between a Calculated Column and a Measure?

options.content: Columns use DAX, Measures use SQL
Columns calculate row-by-row; Measures calculate based on filter context
Measures take up more file space

mcqs.question: In data modeling, what is the most common relationship type between a Fact table and a Dimension table?
What happens by default when you click a data point (like a bar) in a Power BI visual?

options.content: It opens the source data file
It cross-filters or cross-highlights other visuals on the page
It exports the chart to PDF

mcqs.question: Which view in Power BI Desktop is used to manage relationships between tables?
To share a Power BI report with colleagues so they can view it in a web browser, you must:

options.content: Publish it to the Power BI Service
Save it as an HTML file

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid data source for Power BI?
In Power Query, what happens if you make a mistake in data transformation?

options.content: The original data file is permanently corrupted
You must start over from scratch
You can delete or modify the step in the 'Applied Steps' pane
